description_long: This is a small, single-story brick house with a hip roof covered in gray shingles. The exterior walls are constructed from reddish-brown bricks, some of which show signs of weathering and discoloration. The roof features a single brick chimney and a central ridge, with the shingles arranged in a regular, hexagonal pattern. The front facade has a wooden door and two rectangular windows with white frames and decorative upper panes. The windows are symmetrically placed, while the door is offset to one side, and there is visible weathering and some moss or dirt near the base of the walls. The house has a simple, utilitarian design typical of early-to-mid 20th-century suburban or rural homes.
description: A small, single-story brick house with a gray hip roof, chimney, and symmetrically placed white-framed windows. The facade features a wooden door and visible weathering on the brickwork.
description_view_0: The rear view shows a plain brick wall with no windows or doors, and the hip roof with a central ridge and chimney visible.
description_view_1: The side view displays two white-framed windows symmetrically placed on the brick wall, with the chimney and roof ridge visible above.
description_view_2: The opposite rear view mirrors view 0, showing a plain brick wall and the hip roof, with the chimney positioned slightly off-center.
description_view_3: The front view features a wooden door and a single window with a white frame, as well as visible weathering and discoloration on the lower brickwork.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple wooden door with no porch or steps, offset to one side, and minimal decorative elements.
